_comment: Scopes present in scope-allowlist.ts (via the rawapi source) that the Feishu/Lark developer console rejects with 'permission does not exist' on bulk import. Excluded from the console-import list embedded in docs/app-setup_{en,zh}.md. Ground truth: a real bulk-import into a test app (all 226 imported, exactly these were rejected). Capabilities are unaffected — coarse :write scopes and correctly-spelled variants remain in the allowlist. Re-verify after each lark-cli bump (see docs/skills/bump-lark-cli.md Step 6c).

misspelled_or_aggregate._comment: Not real console permission points — wrong spelling or a bare aggregate. Correct forms remain in the list (e.g. bitable:app, im:resource, mail:user_mailbox:readonly, calendar:calendar:readonly).

task_fine_grained._comment: Fine-grained task actions the console folds into the coarse task:*:write (which remains). Exception: task:personnel:writeonly has no coarse counterpart, but no user-facing tool depends on it — it is rawapi-only.

whitelist_gated._comment: Real scope but whitelist-restricted; ordinary apps cannot request it. The lark_slides_screenshot tool maps to wiki:node:read via shortcut, not this scope, so dropping it costs nothing.
